name: Tabs
description: Local tablist for switching between related sibling views or content sections without leaving the current page.

props.name: items
props.description: Tab items to render. Each item has value, label, optional icon, optional slotContent, and optional disabled state.

props.name: onChange
props.description: Called when a tab is selected.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional class name for the root tablist.

states.rationale: Unselected enabled tabs use the shared hover overlay token. Selected tabs use base surface, active border, medium text, and small shadow for pill tabs. Focus uses the shared focus-ring token.

keyboard.Tab: Move focus to the selected tab in the tablist
keyboard.ArrowLeft: Select and focus the previous enabled tab
keyboard.ArrowUp: Select and focus the previous enabled tab
keyboard.ArrowRight: Select and focus the next enabled tab
keyboard.ArrowDown: Select and focus the next enabled tab
keyboard.Home: Select and focus the first enabled tab
keyboard.End: Select and focus the last enabled tab

propertyMapping.note: Figma visibility toggle. In code, item count and order come from the items array.

Visual-only Figma property. Selected maps to value === item.value; hover and focus come from CSS pseudo-classes.
Figma editable text maps to items[].label.

useWhen: Switching between sibling views in the same local context
Entity detail pages with compact subviews
Settings or admin pages with related sections
Dense interfaces that need a low-height view switcher
doNotUseFor: Global navigation - use Sidebar or TopBar
Primary or secondary actions - use Button

knownDeviations.description: Figma exposes tab-item as a separate component set, but React does not export TabItem. Tab items are configured through the Tabs items array.
Figma tab-group exposes fixed tab1/tab2/tab3 visibility toggles. React uses a dynamic items array for count, order, labels, icons, slot content, and disabled state.
Tabs currently does not own tab panels or panel IDs, so tab buttons do not set aria-controls.
L4 tab foreground tokens exist in tokens.json, but current CSS uses semantic text tokens to match Figma text/primary bindings.
